content.problemStatement:
A tree of NNN nodes and N−1N - 1N−1 edges is a connected undirected graph without cycles. A given tree of NNN nodes is called a **star** if there exists a node XXX such that node XXX is directly connected to each of the other N−1N - 1N−1 nodes.

Now, for any given tree TTT we define f(T)f(T)f(T) as the **minimum** number of needed operations to convert the tree into a **star** with the following operations:


Firstly, choose a pair of nodes (U,V)(U, V)(U,V) such that U≠VU \ne VU=V.
Consider the set AAA of all edges (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y) such that XXX belongs to the path U−VU - VU−V (and X≠UX \ne UX=U), and YYY does **not** belong to the path U−VU - VU−V. Choose any subset BBB of AAA.
For all (X,Y)∈B(X, Y) \in B(X,Y)∈B, replace the edge (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y) with the edge (U,Y)(U, Y)(U,Y). It can be proven that TTT still remains a tree.
Find the minimum value of f(T)f(T)f(T) over all spanning trees of a given connected undirected graph GGG with NNN nodes.

Note that a spanning tree of a graph GGG is a subset of edges of the graph such that they form a tree of NNN nodes and N−1N - 1N−1 edges.


Input Format

The first line contains one integer, TTT the number of test cases.
The second line contains one integer, NNN the number of nodes in the graph.
The ithi^{th}ith of the next NNN lines contains the binary string SiS_iSi​ of size NNN, where S(i,j)=1S_{(i, j)} = 1S(i,j)​=1 implies there is an edge between nodes iii and jjj, and otherwise, no edge between nodes iii and jjj.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer, the minimum of f(T)f(T)f(T) over all spanning trees of a given graph GGG.
constraints: The sum of 2N2^N2N over all test cases does not exceed 2182^{18}218.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 : The given graph is a tree, and infact it is also already a star (node 222 is our required central node XXX and you can check node 222 is connected to both 111 and 333).
Test case 222 : The given graph looks like
 
We can consider one spanning tree of this graph by removing the edge (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4) and keeping the other edges (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2), (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) and (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4).
 
Choose U=1,V=2U = 1, V = 2U=1,V=2, then the path U−VU - VU−V contains nodes 111 and 222. Now, A={(2,4)}A = \{(2, 4)\}A={(2,4)} as it is the only edge satisfying the condition. Note that we won't consider (4,2)(4, 2)(4,2) in AAA as order is important for the definition of AAA.
Now, we choose B={(2,4)}B = \{(2, 4)\}B={(2,4)} and perform the operation replacing the edge (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4) with (1,4)(1, 4)(1,4). This transforms our graph to a star (represented below) as node 111 is the required central node which is connected to every other node.
